summary.zh: 1648年，《威斯特伐利亚和约》结束三十年战争，欧洲主权国家体系逐步成形；与此同时，清朝入关后扩张、莫卧儿帝国与萨法维帝国仍具强势、奥斯曼帝国维持横跨欧亚非的大国地位，日本处于德川幕府统治之下，东南亚、美洲与非洲则由区域王国、帝国、部族联盟与欧洲殖民政权并存构成复杂格局。
summary.en: In 1648, the Peace of Westphalia ended the Thirty Years' War and helped formalize the European sovereign state system; meanwhile, the Qing expanded after entering China proper, the Mughal and Safavid Empires remained powerful, the Ottoman Empire still spanned Europe, Asia, and Africa, Tokugawa rule governed Japan, and Southeast Asia, the Americas, and Africa were shaped by a complex mix of regional kingdoms, empires, tribal confederacies, and European colonial regimes.

governmentForm.zh: 战时流亡型君主专制政体，沿袭明朝皇帝—内阁—六部体制，但因长期迁徙与军事压力而高度简化
governmentForm.en: A wartime refugee-style autocratic monarchy preserving the Ming emperor–Grand Secretariat–Six Ministries framework, but heavily simplified by continual flight and military pressure

socialStructure.zh: 社会仍以士农工商的明代秩序为名义框架，但在战乱中军镇、地方豪强、海上商人和流亡官僚的地位显著上升
socialStructure.en: Society still nominally followed the Ming order of scholar-officials, peasants, artisans, and merchants, but warfare greatly elevated the importance of military commanders, local strongmen, maritime merchants, and refugee officials

rulingClass.zh: 流亡宗室、文官士大夫、地方军阀化将领与依附朝廷的地方绅士共同构成统治核心
rulingClass.en: The ruling core consisted of the exiled imperial clan, civil scholar-officials, increasingly warlord-like generals, and local gentry tied to the court

succession.zh: 皇位原则上依明朝宗法与皇室嫡系继承，但在国难中受拥立、军事实力与在位朝臣支持所强烈影响
succession.en: The throne was in principle inherited according to Ming dynastic patrilineal succession, but in practice under crisis it was strongly shaped by enthronement politics, military backing, and court support

structure.zh: 中央政府名义上保有内阁、六部、都察院及若干寺监机构，但在1648年实际运作范围局限于两广、西南若干据点及可联系的地方行政网络；朝廷频繁迁移，诏令执行依赖地方督抚、总兵与藩镇协力
structure.en: The central government nominally retained the Grand Secretariat, Six Ministries, the Censorate, and certain court offices, but by 1648 its effective reach was confined to parts of Guangdong, Guangxi, and southwestern strongholds with whatever local administrative networks remained accessible; the court moved frequently, and implementation depended on provincial officials, regional commanders, and semi-autonomous military authorities

function.zh: 起草诏令、协调六部事务、处理军政机务，是皇帝与百官之间的主要文书中枢
function.en: Drafted edicts, coordinated the Six Ministries, and handled high civil-military affairs as the main documentary link between emperor and bureaucracy

function.zh: 管理官员任免、考课与名义上的铨选，但战时多受派系和军政现实左右
function.en: Managed appointments, evaluations, and nominal personnel selection, though wartime realities and factionalism often overrode procedure

function.zh: 负责田赋、盐课、仓储、军饷筹措与钱粮转运，是战时最关键的财政部门
function.en: Oversaw land tax, salt revenue, granaries, military provisioning, and fiscal transport, making it the most critical wartime ministry

function.zh: 主持祭祀、册封、外交文书与科举残存事务，以维持朝廷正统象征
function.en: Handled ritual, investiture, diplomatic correspondence, and the remnants of examination matters to preserve dynastic legitimacy

function.zh: 管理兵籍、调兵、军报与将领任命，但对各地实力将领的控制有限
function.en: Managed troop registers, deployments, military reports, and appointments, though it had only limited control over powerful regional commanders

function.zh: 审理重大案件、核覆刑狱，维系战时最低限度的法统
function.en: Reviewed major cases and criminal judgments, maintaining a minimum level of legal legitimacy in wartime

function.zh: 负责军需制造、城防修筑、舟船征调与道路桥梁修缮
function.en: Handled military manufacturing, fortifications, requisition of boats, and repair of roads and bridges

function.zh: 监察百官、弹劾失职、巡按地方，但在时局崩坏中更多承担政治监督与道义谏诤功能
function.en: Supervised officials, impeached misconduct, and inspected local administration, though amid collapse it functioned increasingly as a body of political oversight and moral remonstrance

localAdmin.zh: 地方仍沿用省、府、州、县层级名义体系，但大量地区由督抚、总兵、土司、乡绅与地方武装共同治理；税粮征收和司法执行高度不均衡，许多县治仅保有象征性朝廷联系
localAdmin.en: Locally the polity still nominally followed the province–prefecture–subprefecture–county hierarchy, but many areas were effectively governed jointly by provincial commanders, military generals, native chieftains, gentry, and local armed groups; tax collection and judicial enforcement were highly uneven, and many counties retained only symbolic ties to the court

legalSystem.zh: 法律以《大明律》与明代成例为基础，辅以战时敕令、军法与地方权宜处分；在前线和动乱地区，军法常凌驾常规司法
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on the Great Ming Code and established Ming precedents, supplemented by wartime edicts, martial regulations, and local expedients; in frontline and disturbed areas military law often superseded ordinary judicial process

taxationSystem.zh: 税制名义上延续一条鞭法后的地丁银征收体制，并辅以盐税、商税、关津、捐输和临时军需摊派；实际因失地、逃亡和军阀截留而征收率偏低
taxationSystem.en: Taxation nominally continued the post-Single Whip system of silver land-and-labor commutation, supplemented by salt taxes, commercial levies, transit dues, contributions, and emergency military exactions; in practice collection rates were low because of territorial loss, displacement, and diversion by commanders

religion.zh: 以儒家国家礼制为正统，民间普遍信奉佛教、道教及地方神祇；战乱中忠义祭祀、护国祈祷与末世性宗教情绪有所增强
religion.en: Orthodox state ritual remained Confucian, while popular religion widely combined Buddhism, Daoism, and local cults; warfare intensified cults of loyalty, prayers for dynastic protection, and apocalyptic religious sentiment

philosophy.zh: 主流思想仍为晚明儒学，兼受王学、经世致用与忠义伦理影响；亡国危机使正统、节义与华夷之辨成为政治思想中心
philosophy.en: Mainstream thought remained late Ming Confucianism, influenced by Wang Yangming learning, practical statecraft, and ethics of loyalty; dynastic crisis made legitimacy, martyrdom, and the distinction between the civilized realm and foreign conquerors central themes

writingSystem.zh: 以文言文为正式书写语言，使用汉字；诏令、公文、奏疏与地方文书皆循明代文书传统
writingSystem.en: Classical Literary Chinese written in Chinese characters remained the formal written medium; edicts, memorials, and local documents followed established Ming documentary conventions

culturalAchievements.zh: 尽管政局危急，南明仍延续晚明文人书画、史论、殉节诗文与遗民著述传统；宫廷与士人努力通过年号、祭礼、诏书和修史意识维系明朝正统记忆
culturalAchievements.en: Despite political crisis, the Southern Ming continued late Ming literati traditions in painting, calligraphy, historical reflection, loyalist poetry, and writings of displaced scholars; court and literati alike used reign titles, rituals, proclamations, and historical consciousness to preserve Ming legitimacy

languageFamily.zh: 汉藏语系汉语族，行政与精英书面语为文言汉语，民间通行粤语、官话、西南官话及其他地方汉语和少数民族语言
languageFamily.en: Sino-Tibetan, Sinitic branch; the administrative and elite written language was Literary Chinese, while spoken varieties included Cantonese, Mandarin forms, Southwestern Mandarin, and numerous other Chinese and minority languages

gdpDescription.zh: 南明控制区经济仍有相当农业与商贸基础，尤其两广与西南部分区域仍能产粮、产糖、采矿并参与海陆贸易，但战乱、迁徙、封锁和财政掠取严重削弱了总产出与市场整合
gdpDescription.en: The Southern Ming-controlled zones still retained a substantial agricultural and commercial base, especially in parts of Guangdong, Guangxi, and the southwest that produced grain, sugar, and minerals and remained connected to overland and maritime trade; however, war, displacement, blockade, and fiscal predation sharply reduced total output and market integration

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、甘蔗与糖业、盐业、手工纺织、陶瓷、金属器制造、木材采办、矿产开采、军需制造与区域转口贸易
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, sugar cultivation and refining, salt production, textile handicrafts, ceramics, metalworking, timber procurement, mining, military supply production, and regional entrepôt trade

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、糖、盐、丝绸、棉布、瓷器、药材、木材、铜铅锡等矿产、纸张、茶及海外输入的香料、硫磺与军需物资
tradeGoods.en: Rice, sugar, salt, silk, cotton cloth, porcelain, medicinal materials, timber, minerals such as copper, lead, and tin, paper, tea, and imported overseas goods including spices, sulfur, and military supplies

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富分化极大。部分商人、盐商和地方豪绅仍保有银两、田产与货栈，但广大农户因征发、兵燹和逃荒而负担沉重，许多家庭以粮食、布匹与人力作为主要可支配资源
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Some merchants, salt dealers, and local gentry still held silver, land, and warehouses, but most peasant households bore heavy burdens from requisitions, warfare, and displacement, with grain, cloth, and labor serving as their main disposable resources

economicSystem.zh: 以农业税基和市场交换并存的晚期传统经济，国家通过税收、征发、官办与军需采购介入经济，但战时越来越依赖地方自筹、商人融资和半私营军供体系
economicSystem.en: A late traditional economy combining an agrarian tax base with market exchange; the state intervened through taxes, requisitions, official production, and military procurement, but wartime conditions made it increasingly dependent on local self-financing, merchant credit, and semi-private supply systems

source.en: Land tax and commuted labor dues
Mining taxes, miscellaneous levies, and confiscations

category.en: Court, ritual, and imperial household expenditure

category.zh: 外交、赏赐与安抚地方势力
category.en: Diplomacy, rewards, and pacification of local power holders

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模有限，且多为分散在行在、地方仓库和将领掌握下的银粮；账面收入与实际可调度现金常有巨大差距
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was limited in size and much of it was dispersed among the itinerant court, local granaries, and stocks controlled by generals; nominal revenue and actually disposable cash often differed greatly

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持抗清战争为中心，强调催征、加派、招商输饷与借助地方绅商捐助；虽力图恢复明制，但长期赤字和军头截留使财政高度脆弱
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on sustaining the war against the Qing, emphasizing urgent collection, surtaxes, merchant-funded grain transport, and contributions from local gentry and traders; although the court sought to restore Ming norms, chronic deficits and diversion by military strongmen made finances extremely fragile

description.zh: 人数最多，装备长枪、刀牌、弓弩及火器混合，战斗力因将领、训练和军饷状况差异极大
description.en: The largest arm, equipped with a mix of spears, sabers and shields, bows, crossbows, and firearms; effectiveness varied enormously by commander, training, and pay

description.zh: 数量有限，主要用于侦察、突击与护卫，在南方地形中作用不及北方骑兵，但仍为精锐成分
description.en: Limited in number, used chiefly for scouting, shock action, and escort duties; less suited to southern terrain than northern cavalry, yet still an elite component

description.zh: 依托江河与沿海水网行动，承担运兵、护粮、封锁与联络任务，是南明维持机动性的关键力量之一
description.en: Operating on rivers, estuaries, and coastal waters, these forces transported troops, protected grain shipments, enforced blockades, and maintained communications, making them crucial to Southern Ming mobility

description.zh: 由边地土司、山地社群和地方寨堡提供，熟悉地形，适于守险和游击，但难以统一指挥
description.en: Provided by native chieftains, upland communities, and local stockades; they knew the terrain well and were useful for defense and guerrilla warfare, but were difficult to command uniformly

commanderInChief.zh: 永历帝名义上为最高统帅，实际由兵部、大学士及李定国等主要将领分掌军务
commanderInChief.en: The Yongli Emperor was the nominal commander-in-chief, while actual military affairs were shared among the Ministry of War, senior grand secretaries, and leading generals such as Li Dingguo

technology.zh: 军备属于17世纪中叶东亚火器时代水平，广泛使用火绳枪、鸟铳、火炮、火箭与传统冷兵器；但兵工生产分散，火药、铅弹、炮械质量与补给稳定性不如清军和部分沿海强藩
technology.en: Military technology stood at the mid-17th-century East Asian gunpowder level, with widespread use of matchlocks, arquebuses, cannon, rockets, and traditional melee weapons; however, arms production was dispersed, and the quality and supply of powder, shot, and artillery were less reliable than those of the Qing and some stronger maritime forces

threats.zh: 首要威胁为满清南下与地方降清势力；其次是内部将领离心、军纪败坏、补给匮乏、地方土司摇摆，以及沿海和内陆交通线遭切断
threats.en: The primary threat was Qing southward expansion and collaborationist local forces; secondary dangers included centrifugal generals, poor discipline, supply shortages, wavering native chieftains, and severed coastal and inland communication lines

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于两广与西南联系地带，因战乱、迁徙、饥荒与疫病而显著流动；朝廷名义统辖人口远大于其实际稳定控制人口
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in Guangdong, Guangxi, and connected southwestern zones, and was highly mobile because of war, displacement, famine, and disease; the court’s nominal population base was far larger than the population it stably controlled in practice

socialClasses.zh: 上层为皇室宗藩、文官士绅与军政大员；中层包括地方绅商、盐商、手工业主与低级吏员；下层为自耕农、佃农、匠户、船民、矿工、士兵与大量流民难民。另有壮、瑶、苗及西南诸族土司体系与汉地行政社会并存
socialClasses.en: The upper strata comprised the imperial house, civil gentry, and senior military-political officials; the middle included local gentry-merchants, salt dealers, workshop owners, and petty clerks; the lower consisted of owner-peasants, tenants, artisans, boat people, miners, soldiers, and large numbers of displaced refugees. In addition, Zhuang, Yao, Miao, and other southwestern native chieftain systems coexisted with the Han administrative order

allies.zh: 无稳定国家级盟友；主要依赖内部抗清势力、郑氏海上力量的有限协同，以及与西南土司、地方豪强和部分边地政权的临时合作
allies.en: It had no stable state-level allies; it relied mainly on internal anti-Qing forces, limited coordination with the Zheng maritime power, and temporary cooperation with southwestern native chieftains, local strongmen, and some frontier polities

enemies.zh: 满清帝国及其地方归附者、降将与协从武装
enemies.en: The Manchu Qing Empire and its local adherents, surrendered generals, and collaborating armed forces

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维持明朝正统名义、争取外部承认、拉拢海上与边地势力并分化清朝阵营为主；但受制于控制区缩小、通信困难与资源匮乏，外交成效有限
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving Ming legitimacy, seeking outside recognition, attracting maritime and frontier support, and splitting the Qing camp; however, shrinking territory, poor communications, and limited resources sharply constrained its effectiveness

keyInnovations.zh: 继承晚明成熟的火器制造、城防工事、印刷出版、商业账簿、远距离银粮运输和海河舟船技术；但1648年的南明更偏重保存既有技术体系而非产生重大新发明
keyInnovations.en: It inherited the late Ming’s mature systems of firearms manufacture, fortification, printing, commercial bookkeeping, long-distance silver-and-grain transport, and river-sea vessel technology; but by 1648 the Southern Ming was more concerned with preserving existing techniques than generating major new inventions

strengths.zh: 仍拥有明朝正统名义、士大夫忠诚网络、华南丰饶地区的部分税源、较强的水网机动能力，以及在地方社会中广泛存在的反清情绪
strengths.en: It still possessed the legitimacy of Ming dynastic continuity, networks of loyal scholar-officials, access to parts of the wealthy south China tax base, strong mobility along riverine waterways, and widespread anti-Qing sentiment in local society

weaknesses.zh: 政权分裂、将领互不统属、财政枯竭、军纪不整、后勤脆弱、地方控制有限，并长期面对清军在兵员组织与战略推进上的优势
weaknesses.en: The polity suffered from fragmentation, commanders lacking unified subordination, fiscal exhaustion, weak discipline, fragile logistics, limited local control, and the long-term Qing advantage in organization and strategic momentum

outlook.zh: 1648年的南明虽仍具抵抗能力，且在若干战区可能出现局部反攻，但整体趋势极为险峻。若不能实现军政统一、稳定税源并形成持久联盟，其生存空间将继续缩小
outlook.en: In 1648 the Southern Ming still retained the capacity to resist, and local counteroffensives were possible in some theaters, but the overall trajectory was extremely precarious. Unless it could unify military and civil authority, stabilize revenue, and build durable alliances, its room for survival would continue to shrink

description.zh: 南明诸政权仍在华南与西南坚持抗清，但内部离散且局势危急。
description.en: Southern Ming regimes still resisted the Qing in South and Southwest China, but remained fragmented and precarious.

governmentForm.zh: 高度中央集权的伊斯兰君主制帝国，以皇帝为核心，通过曼萨卜达里官僚—军役体系统治多民族、多宗教疆域
governmentForm.en: A highly centralized Islamic imperial monarchy centered on the emperor, ruling a multi-ethnic and multi-religious realm through the mansabdari bureaucratic-military system

socialStructure.zh: 社会结构呈明显等级化：皇室与宫廷贵族居上，随后为曼萨卜达尔军政贵族、地方扎明达尔、乌里玛与文人、城市商人和手工业者、乡村农民，以及各类依附劳动者与奴仆；宗教、族群与种姓差异在地方社会中持续发挥作用
socialStructure.en: Society was distinctly hierarchical: the imperial family and court nobility stood at the top, followed by mansabdar military-administrative elites, local zamindars, ulama and literati, urban merchants and artisans, rural cultivators, and various dependent laborers and servants; religious, ethnic, and caste distinctions continued to shape local society

rulingClass.zh: 以皇帝直属的莫卧儿、突厥、波斯、印度穆斯林及拉杰普特贵族组成的宫廷—曼萨卜达尔精英集团为统治核心
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was the court-mansabdar elite composed of imperial Mughal, Turani, Irani, Indian Muslim, and Rajput nobles directly tied to the emperor

succession.zh: 名义上为王朝世袭，但无严格长子继承法，皇子通常通过宫廷联盟与内战竞争皇位
succession.en: Nominally dynastic and hereditary, but without strict primogeniture; imperial princes typically contested the throne through court alliances and civil war

structure.zh: 中央政府由皇帝、御前枢密与诸大臣构成，核心依托财政、省务、军役与宫廷事务等部门，并通过苏巴、省、萨卡尔、帕尔加纳到村社的层级体系实施统治；官职与军阶大体纳入曼萨卜制
structure.en: The central government consisted of the emperor, the privy court, and great ministers, relying on departments for finance, provincial administration, military service, and court affairs; rule was implemented through a hierarchy of subah, sarkar, pargana, and village institutions, with offices and ranks broadly integrated into the mansabdari system

function.zh: 负责帝国财政、土地收入核算、贾吉尔分配、审计与国库管理
function.en: Managed imperial finance, land revenue assessment, jagir assignments, auditing, and treasury administration

function.zh: 负责曼萨卜授予、军官登记、骑兵检阅、俸饷名册与军事人事
function.en: Handled mansab appointments, officer registration, cavalry inspection, payroll rolls, and military personnel affairs

function.zh: 管理宗教捐赠、学术与宗教俸给、部分法官任命及伊斯兰法事务协调
function.en: Oversaw religious endowments, stipends for scholars and clerics, certain judicial appointments, and coordination of Islamic legal affairs

function.zh: 负责宫廷供给、御用作坊、建筑工程、道路园林与皇室财产管理
function.en: Managed court supply, imperial workshops, construction projects, roads and gardens, and crown properties

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以苏巴为一级行省，由苏巴达尔、迪万与法官等相互牵制；下设萨卡尔与帕尔加纳，地方层面依赖卡农戈、阿明、希克达尔、科特瓦尔及村社头人，并与扎明达尔合作征收赋税与维持秩序
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized around the subah as the primary province, where the subahdar, provincial diwan, and qazi checked one another; below were sarkars and parganas, relying on qanungos, amins, shiqdars, kotwals, and village headmen, while cooperating with zamindars to collect revenue and maintain order

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系具有多元性：伊斯兰教法适用于穆斯林个人法与部分刑名领域，皇帝敕令与行政惯例构成重要统治依据，印度教社群和地方习惯法在婚姻、继承、村社与种姓事务中仍被广泛承认
legalSystem.en: The legal system was plural: Islamic law applied to Muslim personal law and parts of criminal justice, imperial decrees and administrative practice formed a major basis of rule, and Hindu community norms and local customary law remained widely recognized in marriage, inheritance, village, and caste matters

taxationSystem.zh: 财政核心为土地税，依据作物、面积、产量与地区习惯进行评估，多以现金折算征收；此外还有关税、铸币与市场税、盐税、港口税及附加徭役与地方摊派，地方扎明达尔常保留部分征收份额
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal core was land revenue, assessed by crop, area, output, and local practice, often commuted into cash; additional revenues came from customs, mint and market dues, salt duties, port taxes, and supplementary local imposts and labor obligations, with zamindars often retaining a share of collections

religion.zh: 帝国官方政治文化以逊尼派伊斯兰教为中心，但臣民多数为印度教徒；宫廷容纳波斯化伊斯兰传统，同时与苏菲派、什叶派来客、耆那教、锡克教及多种地方宗教实践长期共存
religion.en: The empire's official political culture was centered on Sunni Islam, though the majority of subjects were Hindu; the court embodied a Persianate Islamic tradition while coexisting over time with Sufi orders, Shia visitors, Jainism, Sikhism, and numerous local religious practices

philosophy.zh: 思想领域以波斯化君主政治观、伊斯兰法学、苏菲神秘主义和印度本土伦理传统并存；宫廷强调王权庄严、正义治理与宇宙秩序，文人世界则兼容阿拉伯-波斯学术与梵文、地方语言知识
philosophy.en: Intellectual life combined Persianate theories of kingship, Islamic jurisprudence, Sufi mysticism, and indigenous Indian ethical traditions; the court stressed royal majesty, just rule, and cosmic order, while literate culture accommodated both Arabo-Persian scholarship and Sanskrit and vernacular learning

writingSystem.zh: 行政与高雅文学主要使用波斯文，采用阿拉伯字母的波斯书写体系；阿拉伯文用于宗教学术，地方社会广泛使用天城文、古木基文、孟加拉文、古吉拉特文及其他地方文字
writingSystem.en: Persian was the principal language of administration and high literature, written in the Perso-Arabic script; Arabic was used for religious scholarship, while regional society widely employed Devanagari, Gurmukhi, Bengali, Gujarati, and other local scripts

culturalAchievements.zh: 沙贾汗时期是莫卧儿建筑与宫廷艺术高峰之一，代表包括泰姬陵、德里红堡、贾玛清真寺及精致的庭园、珠宝、玉器、细密画和书法；宫廷礼仪、织物美学与城市景观对欧亚世界影响深远
culturalAchievements.en: The reign of Shah Jahan marked one of the high points of Mughal architecture and court arts, exemplified by the Taj Mahal, the Red Fort in Delhi, the Jama Masjid, and refined gardens, jewelry, jade work, miniatures, and calligraphy; court ritual, textile aesthetics, and urban design had broad influence across Eurasia

languageFamily.zh: 帝国为多语言文明：宫廷主导语言属印欧语系伊朗语支的波斯语，民间主体语言多属印欧语系印度-雅利安语支，另有突厥语与达罗毗荼语广泛分布
languageFamily.en: The empire was multilingual: the dominant court language, Persian, belonged to the Indo-European Iranian branch, while most popular languages were Indo-Aryan, with Turkic and Dravidian languages also widely present

unit.en: Mughal rupees per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 1648年前后的莫卧儿帝国很可能是世界上总量最大的经济体之一，依托高人口、高农业产出、发达纺织业与广域市场网络。其经济规模极大，但人均收入虽在前近代大国中不低，仍受传统农业社会生产率上限制约
gdpDescription.en: Around 1648 the Mughal Empire was likely one of the largest economies in the world by aggregate output, supported by a vast population, high agricultural production, a developed textile sector, and extensive market networks. Its total economic scale was enormous, though per capita income, while respectable by premodern standards, remained constrained by the productivity limits of an agrarian society

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、棉纺织、丝织、靛蓝生产、糖业、造船、金属加工、石材建筑、手工奢侈品制造、马匹与军需供应
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, cotton textiles, silk weaving, indigo production, sugar processing, shipbuilding, metalworking, stone construction, luxury crafts, and horse and military supply

tradeGoods.zh: 棉布、细布、丝绸、靛蓝、糖、香料转口品、鸦片、盐硝、宝石、玉器、纸张、金属器、地毯，以及进口的白银、铜、战马、珊瑚和部分奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Cotton cloth, muslins, silk, indigo, sugar, re-exported spices, opium, saltpeter, gemstones, jade work, paper, metal goods, carpets, and imported silver, copper, warhorses, coral, and select luxury wares

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富分化显著：城市商人与大地主可积累大量现银、珠宝、土地收益权与库存商品，而普通农户财富主要体现为耕畜、农具、粮食储备与有限首饰；农村社会普遍易受歉收、赋役与债务冲击
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal: urban merchants and major landholders could accumulate large holdings in silver, jewels, land-revenue rights, and inventories, while ordinary cultivator wealth consisted mainly of draft animals, tools, grain reserves, and limited jewelry; rural society remained vulnerable to bad harvests, taxes, and debt

unit.en: Mughal rupees per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 经济体制属以农业地租与国家征收为基础的前近代商业化帝国经济。国家通过土地税、贾吉尔分封、铸币与关税体系汲取资源，同时依赖城市市场、商帮、金融票据和跨洋贸易网络维持流通
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a commercialized premodern imperial economy grounded in agrarian rent and state extraction. The state drew resources through land revenue, jagir assignments, minting, and customs, while circulation depended on urban markets, merchant communities, credit instruments, and maritime trade networks

source.zh: 皇家领地、作坊与铸币收益
source.en: Crown lands, workshops, and mint revenues
Market dues, miscellaneous taxes, and confiscations

category.zh: 军队、曼萨卜俸给与边防
category.en: Army, mansab salaries, and frontier defense
Court, royal household, and ceremonial expenditure

category.zh: 建筑工程、道路园林与宗教捐赐
category.en: Construction, roads and gardens, and religious patronage

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库储备雄厚，既包括现银与金币，也包括珠宝、器物、贵金属储藏和可动用的皇室财物。沙贾汗时期因长期积累、商业繁荣与广泛征收而保持较强财政承受力，但巨额建筑和宫廷支出同样十分可观
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was substantial, consisting not only of coined silver and gold but also jewels, precious objects, bullion stores, and mobilizable royal assets. Under Shah Jahan, long accumulation, commercial prosperity, and extensive revenue extraction sustained strong fiscal capacity, though grand construction and court expenses were likewise immense

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持高效土地征收、稳定银本位货币、保障军政贵族俸给与展示皇权威仪；在丰年追求高额征收与建筑投资，在局部危机时则依赖调拨、减免或临时性摊派调节
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized efficient land-revenue collection, maintenance of a stable silver-based currency, payment of the military-administrative nobility, and the display of imperial grandeur; in prosperous years it pursued heavy extraction and building investment, while in localized crises it relied on transfers, remissions, or temporary imposts

description.zh: 骑兵是莫卧儿军力核心，由皇帝直属精锐、曼萨卜达尔配属骑兵与地方贵族部队构成，擅长机动打击、追击与战场威慑
description.en: Cavalry formed the core of Mughal power, consisting of imperial elite units, mansabdar contingents, and forces attached by local nobles, excelling in mobility, shock action, pursuit, and battlefield intimidation

description.zh: 步兵包括火绳枪手、弓箭手、持矛兵与守城部队，整体地位低于骑兵，但在围城、守备与火力支援中不可或缺
description.en: Infantry included matchlock men, archers, spearmen, and garrison troops; though socially less prestigious than cavalry, they were indispensable for sieges, defense, and fire support

description.zh: 炮兵拥有攻城重炮、野战炮与火箭等装备，并配有筑垒、爆破和架桥人员，是莫卧儿攻城作战的重要优势
description.en: Artillery included heavy siege guns, field pieces, and rockets, supported by specialists in fortification, demolition, and bridging, giving the Mughals a major advantage in siege warfare

name.zh: 战象、宫廷卫队与地方辅助军
name.en: War Elephants, Palace Guard, and Auxiliary Forces

description.zh: 包括象队、皇宫禁卫、边疆守备军及由扎明达尔和盟属提供的各类辅助兵，主要用于辎重、威仪、地区镇压与补充兵力
description.en: These included elephant corps, palace guards, frontier garrisons, and various auxiliaries supplied by zamindars and subordinate allies, used for logistics, prestige, regional suppression, and manpower reinforcement

commanderInChief.zh: 沙贾汗皇帝兼最高统帅，军务由米尔·巴赫希统筹
commanderInChief.en: The emperor Shah Jahan served as commander-in-chief, with military affairs coordinated by the Mir Bakhshi

technology.zh: 军事技术处于早期近代火器时代高水平，综合运用火绳枪、铸炮、攻城工程、战象与传统骑射；但在战术制度化、海军建设与持续火器操典方面，不如部分同时代西欧强国那样系统化
technology.en: Military technology stood at a high early modern gunpowder level, combining matchlocks, cast artillery, siege engineering, war elephants, and traditional mounted archery; however, it was less systematized than some contemporary Western European powers in tactics, naval development, and sustained firearms drill

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自德干诸苏丹国与南方扩张、阿富汗与西北边疆不稳、波斯萨法维帝国在坎大哈方向的压力，以及地方扎明达尔叛乱和潜在皇室继承战争
threats.en: Major threats came from the Deccan sultanates and southern expansion, instability in Afghanistan and the northwestern frontier, Safavid pressure in the Kandahar direction, and rebellions by local zamindars along with the latent danger of dynastic succession wars

populationDescription.zh: 莫卧儿帝国是17世纪世界人口最多的政治体之一，人口高度集中于恒河平原、旁遮普、孟加拉与印度北部农业核心区，南亚内部区域差异显著
populationDescription.en: The Mughal Empire was one of the most populous polities in the seventeenth-century world, with population concentrated especially in the Gangetic plain, Punjab, Bengal, and the agrarian core zones of northern India, though internal regional variation was considerable

socialClasses.zh: 主要社会阶层包括皇室与宫廷贵族、曼萨卜达尔与军政官员、扎明达尔与地方领主、乌里玛与文人、商人金融家、城市工匠与作坊劳动者、农民、牧民、仆役及边缘化群体；种姓与宗教身份在许多地区与经济阶层交织
socialClasses.en: Major social classes included the imperial family and court nobility, mansabdars and military-administrative officials, zamindars and local lords, ulama and literati, merchants and financiers, urban artisans and workshop laborers, peasants, pastoralists, servants, and marginalized groups; caste and religious identities intersected with economic status in many regions

allies.zh: 莫卧儿帝国1648年并无现代意义上的固定同盟体系，但与部分拉杰普特邦国、若干地方属邦及印度洋商贸伙伴保持合作关系，并与欧洲海上公司在贸易上维持务实往来
allies.en: In 1648 the Mughal Empire had no fixed alliance system in the modern sense, but maintained cooperative relations with certain Rajput states, various subordinate polities, and Indian Ocean trading partners, while dealing pragmatically with European maritime companies in commerce

enemies.zh: 主要对手为萨法维伊朗在坎大哈问题上的竞争势力，以及德干地区尚未完全臣服的苏丹国和周期性反叛的边疆与地方势力
enemies.en: Its principal adversaries were Safavid Iran in the contest over Kandahar, as well as the not yet fully subdued Deccan sultanates and periodically rebellious frontier and local forces

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护西北边疆安全、控制德干、保护印度洋贸易通道和彰显皇帝普世威望为核心；对海上欧洲人多采取许可贸易、限制筑堡和必要时施压的务实策略
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on securing the northwestern frontier, controlling the Deccan, protecting Indian Ocean trade routes, and projecting universal imperial prestige; toward European maritime powers it generally followed a pragmatic strategy of permitting trade, restricting fortification, and applying pressure when needed

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟银币铸造体系、大规模石造穹顶与红砂岩—白大理石建筑工艺、精细纺织与染色技术、火绳枪与攻城炮运用、园林水工与城市规划、金融汇票与长距离商贸结算网络
keyInnovations.en: A mature silver coinage system, large-scale stone dome construction and red sandstone-white marble architecture, refined textile and dyeing techniques, use of matchlocks and siege artillery, garden hydraulics and urban planning, and financial bills of exchange with long-distance commercial settlement networks

strengths.zh: 帝国的优势在于庞大人口与税基、发达农业与纺织业、雄厚国库、华丽而高效的宫廷政治文化、强大的骑兵与炮兵传统，以及覆盖欧亚和印度洋的商业联系
strengths.en: The empire's strengths lay in its vast population and tax base, developed agriculture and textile industry, rich treasury, splendid yet effective court political culture, powerful cavalry and artillery traditions, and commercial connections spanning Eurasia and the Indian Ocean

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点包括对高强度土地征收的依赖、地方扎明达尔和曼萨卜达尔体系的离心倾向、宗教与地区差异造成的治理摩擦、海军薄弱，以及王位继承缺乏明确规则所带来的潜在内战风险
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included dependence on heavy land extraction, centrifugal tendencies within the zamindar and mansabdar system, governing frictions arising from religious and regional diversity, weak naval power, and the risk of civil war created by the lack of a clear succession rule

outlook.zh: 1648年的莫卧儿帝国正处于繁荣巅峰，短期内仍将保持南亚首强地位；但若德干战争扩大、财政压力上升并爆发皇室继承冲突，其长期稳定性将受到严重挑战
outlook.en: In 1648 the Mughal Empire stood near the height of its prosperity and was likely to remain the foremost power in South Asia in the short term; yet if Deccan wars expanded, fiscal pressure rose, and a dynastic succession struggle erupted, its long-term stability would face serious strain

description.zh: 莫卧儿帝国在沙贾汗统治下仍处高峰，是当时世界最富庶政权之一。
description.en: Under Shah Jahan, the Mughal Empire remained near its zenith and was among the wealthiest states in the world.

governmentForm.zh: 由满洲征服王朝统治的中央集权皇帝官僚帝国，兼具八旗军事贵族体制与明代文官制度
governmentForm.en: A centralized imperial bureaucratic empire ruled by a Manchu conquest dynasty, combining the Eight Banner military-aristocratic order with inherited Ming civil administration

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈严格等级化。统治核心为皇室、宗室、旗人贵族与满洲八旗，其次为蒙古与汉军旗人；在广大内地则延续士、农、工、商的传统秩序，地方士绅、举人、进士与旧明官僚在新朝整合中扮演关键中介角色。军户、匠户、民户及边疆部众身份差异明显。
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly hierarchical. At the core stood the imperial clan, banner aristocracy, and Manchu Eight Banners, followed by Mongol and Han Bannermen; across China proper the traditional order of scholar-officials, peasants, artisans, and merchants continued, with local gentry, degree-holders, and former Ming officials serving as crucial intermediaries in the new regime’s consolidation. Distinctions among military households, artisan households, commoner households, and frontier peoples remained important.

rulingClass.zh: 爱新觉罗宗室、满洲八旗贵族、议政王大臣、内院与六部高官，以及被重新任用的汉族文官精英
rulingClass.en: The Aisin Gioro imperial clan, Manchu Eight Banner nobility, deliberative princes and ministers, top officials of the inner court and Six Ministries, and re-employed Han Chinese civil elites

succession.zh: 皇位由爱新觉罗宗室内部世袭继承，原则上由皇帝指定或宫廷共议确认，实际受摄政、宗室势力与宫廷政治影响极大
succession.en: The throne was hereditary within the Aisin Gioro house, in principle designated by the emperor or confirmed through court consensus, though in practice deeply influenced by regents, princely power, and palace politics

structure.zh: 1648年的清廷仍处在入关后早期整合阶段。中央政务以承袭明制的六部为骨架，同时保留满洲议政王大臣会议、内三院等征服王朝机构；在许多衙门中实行满汉并用，以确保旗人控制与汉地治理能力并存。
structure.en: In 1648 the Qing court remained in an early post-conquest phase of consolidation. Central administration was built upon the Ming-derived Six Ministries, while preserving conquest-dynasty institutions such as the Deliberative Council of Princes and Ministers and the Three Inner Courts; many offices operated with parallel Manchu and Han personnel to combine banner control with effective governance of China proper.

function.zh: 掌文官任免、考课、品秩与地方官调补
function.en: Managed appointments, evaluations, ranks, and transfers of civil officials

function.zh: 掌田赋、漕运、户籍、仓储与财政收支
function.en: Oversaw land tax, grain transport, household registration, granaries, and fiscal income and expenditure

function.zh: 掌典礼、祭祀、朝贡关系、学校与科举相关事务
function.en: Handled ritual, sacrifices, tributary relations, schools, and examination-related affairs

function.zh: 掌绿营军政、军籍、驿传与部分军需协调，惟八旗核心军务常由旗务体系与议政机构主导
function.en: Managed Green Standard military administration, registers, courier stations, and some logistical coordination, though core Banner military affairs were often directed by banner institutions and deliberative bodies

function.zh: 掌审刑、律例、复核重案与监狱事务
function.en: Administered judicial review, legal codes, review of major cases, and prison affairs

function.zh: 掌营建、河工、城防工事、军械制造与官方手工业作坊
function.en: Oversaw construction, river works, urban fortifications, arms production, and state workshops

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政大体承袭明代省、府、州、县体系，但战时区划与实际控制仍不稳定。已控制地区由总督、巡抚、布政使、按察使及府县官治理；新附州县常由降官、荐举士绅或临时军政机构接管。辽东、直隶、山东、山西、河南等地整合较深，江南、两湖、西南则仍处于推进过程中。
localAdmin.en: Local administration largely followed the Ming provincial-prefectural-subprefectural-county framework, though wartime jurisdictions and effective control remained fluid. In secured regions, governance was exercised by governors-general, governors, provincial commissioners, judicial commissioners, and prefectural and county magistrates; newly submitted territories were often managed by surrendered officials, recommended local gentry, or temporary military-administrative organs. Liaodong, Zhili, Shandong, Shanxi, and Henan were more firmly integrated, while the Lower Yangzi, the central south, and the southwest were still under active consolidation.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以《大明律》及其清廷修订、敕令和条例为基础，保持中国成文法与皇权裁断并行的传统。旗人与民人、满汉之间在司法上存在一定差别；重大案件需层层复核，政治与军事叛乱案件往往从严处置。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on the Great Ming Code together with Qing revisions, edicts, and supplementary regulations, preserving the Chinese tradition of written law combined with imperial discretion. Some judicial distinctions existed between Bannermen and civilians and between Manchu and Han populations; major cases underwent layered review, while political and military rebellion cases were commonly punished with great severity.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自田赋、丁银、漕粮、盐课、关税与杂税。入关初期，清廷一面沿袭明代黄册与赋役传统，一面在战区采取减免、招抚与恢复征收并行策略，以尽快恢复农业与合法税源。
taxationSystem.en: State revenue derived chiefly from land taxes, poll levies in silver, grain tribute, salt taxes, customs duties, and miscellaneous imposts. In the early post-conquest years the Qing both inherited Ming household and fiscal practices and adopted a mixed policy of remissions, pacification measures, and restored collection in war-torn zones to revive agriculture and secure legitimate revenue.

religion.zh: 国家层面兼容满洲萨满传统、藏传佛教礼遇、汉地儒家祭祀与民间佛道信仰。皇室仍保留萨满祭天祭祖传统，而在中原统治正当性上则强调奉天承运、尊孔崇儒。
religion.en: At the state level religion was plural, combining Manchu shamanic traditions, patronage of Tibetan Buddhism, Confucian state ritual in China proper, and widespread popular Buddhist and Daoist worship. The imperial house retained shamanic rites to heaven and ancestors while stressing Confucian orthodoxy and Heaven’s mandate to legitimize rule over China.

philosophy.zh: 主导思想仍为儒家政治伦理，尤其重视忠、孝、名分与秩序；同时实务治理中强调法制、军功与征服王朝的族群秩序。晚明以来的理学与经世之学仍深刻影响士人。
philosophy.en: Confucian political ethics remained intellectually dominant, especially loyalty, filiality, hierarchy, and social order; practical rule also emphasized legal discipline, military merit, and the ethnic ordering of a conquest dynasty. Neo-Confucian learning and late-Ming statecraft scholarship continued to shape the literati.

writingSystem.zh: 官方使用汉字与满文双重书写体系，重要诏令、档案与军政文书常见满汉并行；蒙古文亦在特定边政与盟旗事务中使用。
writingSystem.en: The state employed a dual writing system of Chinese characters and Manchu script, with major edicts, archives, and military-administrative documents often issued in both languages; Mongolian was also used in certain frontier and banner affairs.

culturalAchievements.zh: 1648年的文化成就主要体现在制度承接与文化整合，而非后世高峰式编纂工程。清廷已开始接收明代典章、图籍、工艺和宫廷艺术传统，北京宫廷礼制、书法、档案编修、织造与兵器制造都在恢复与重建；同时北方宫廷文化与江南文人文化正在逐步接轨。
culturalAchievements.en: In 1648 cultural achievement lay chiefly in institutional continuity and cultural integration rather than in the grand compilations of later reigns. The Qing had already begun absorbing Ming ceremonial norms, libraries, crafts, and court artistic traditions; palace ritual, calligraphy, archival work, textile production, and weapons manufacture in Beijing were being restored and rebuilt, while northern court culture was gradually connecting with Jiangnan literati culture.

languageFamily.zh: 统治核心语言属通古斯语系满语，帝国主要行政与社会通用语言为汉语官话及各汉语方言；并涵盖蒙古语、藏缅语族与多种边疆语言环境
languageFamily.en: The ruling core language was Manchu of the Tungusic family, while the empire’s principal administrative and social lingua franca was Chinese, especially Mandarin and regional Sinitic varieties; Mongolic, Tibeto-Burman, and other frontier language environments were also present

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 1648年的清帝国经济总体规模极大，基础仍是高度发达的农业经济与区域市场网络，但由于统一战争尚未完成，华南、江南与西南部分地区生产和税收恢复不均。北方核心区已在迅速恢复，江南财富带则对未来财政与商业繁荣具有决定性意义。
gdpDescription.en: The Qing economy in 1648 was immense in aggregate size, resting on a highly developed agrarian base and regional market networks, but because unification was still incomplete, production and taxation in parts of the south, Lower Yangzi, and southwest recovered unevenly. The northern core was rebounding rapidly, while the wealthy Jiangnan zone was crucial to future fiscal and commercial prosperity.

mainIndustries.zh: 粮食农业、棉麻纺织、丝织、瓷器、盐业、冶铁、造纸、漕运、手工业兵器制造与区域商业贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain agriculture, cotton and hemp textiles, silk weaving, porcelain, salt production, ironworking, papermaking, grain transport, handicraft arms manufacture, and regional commerce

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、小麦、粟、棉布、丝绸、瓷器、茶、盐、铁器、皮毛、人参、药材与漆器
tradeGoods.en: Rice, wheat, millet, cotton cloth, silk, porcelain, tea, salt, iron goods, furs, ginseng, medicinal materials, and lacquerware

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距极大。江南地主、盐商、典当商与大商人积累了显著财富，而华北战乱地区与新征服区域普通农户多处于低积蓄或负债状态。旗人家庭往往依赖俸饷、赏赐与编旗资源，城乡手工业者与小商贩处于中低层收入带。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Jiangnan landlords, salt merchants, pawn brokers, and large traders accumulated considerable riches, while ordinary farming households in war-damaged north China and newly conquered zones often had little savings or were indebted. Banner households commonly relied on stipends, grants, and banner privileges, while urban artisans and petty traders occupied lower-middle income strata.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以自耕与佃作农业为基础、由国家税收和市场交换联结的前近代商业化农业经济。政府并不实行现代意义上的自由贸易政策，而是在税课、盐业、漕运、关津和战时征发中保有强力干预；同时依赖地方市场、商帮与士绅网络维持流通。
economicSystem.en: A premodern commercialized agrarian economy based on peasant cultivation and tenancy, linked by state taxation and market exchange. The government did not practice free trade in the modern sense, but exercised strong intervention through taxes, salt administration, grain transport, customs, and wartime requisitioning, while relying on local markets, merchant groups, and gentry networks to sustain circulation.

source.zh: 漕粮折银与仓储相关收入
source.en: Commuted grain tribute and granary-related income
Miscellaneous taxes, confiscations, and extraordinary levies

category.en: Eight Banner and Green Standard military costs
Court, imperial clan, and ceremonial spending
River works, urban works, and military manufacturing

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库存银并不宽裕。入关与连年征战带来巨额军需，且许多新占地区账册未清、赋源未稳。北京中央库储维持运转能力，但相当部分财富以粮、布匹、军械、马匹和地方仓储形式存在，而非完全体现为可自由调度的现银。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not abundant. Entry into China proper and continuous campaigning imposed heavy military demands, and in many newly occupied regions fiscal registers remained unsettled and revenue streams insecure. The central stores in Beijing could sustain operations, but much state wealth existed in grain, cloth, weapons, horses, and provincial stocks rather than as fully disposable cash silver.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持战争、安抚地方和恢复赋役为中心。清廷倾向保留明代税制框架，避免在初期进行激进制度革新，以便争取士绅合作并稳定税基；同时通过赏赐、减免与选择性追征并用，兼顾征服成本与合法性建设。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on sustaining war, pacifying local society, and restoring taxation. The Qing generally preferred to preserve the Ming fiscal framework rather than impose radical early reform, thereby winning gentry cooperation and stabilizing the tax base; at the same time it combined rewards, remissions, and selective recovery of arrears to balance conquest costs with political legitimacy.

description.zh: 帝国统治核心武装，由满洲旗人组成，兼具军事、社会与政治功能，驻北京与各战略要地
description.en: The core military force of the dynasty, composed of Manchu Bannermen and serving military, social, and political functions, stationed in Beijing and key strategic centers

description.zh: 由归附蒙古诸部构成，擅长骑射与机动作战，是北方与边疆军事的重要组成部分
description.en: Formed from allied Mongol groups, skilled in mounted archery and mobile warfare, and important for northern and frontier operations

description.zh: 以早期归附汉人军户为主，兼具火器、攻城与守城能力，是清初南下战争的重要力量
description.en: Composed mainly of early-submitted Han military households, with strengths in firearms, siege, and garrison warfare, and a major force in the Qing southern campaigns

description.zh: 以归降明军与后续招募汉人部队为主体，负责地方驻防、治安、围剿与大规模地面作战，数量远超旗兵
description.en: Made up chiefly of surrendered Ming troops and subsequent Han recruits, responsible for local garrison duty, security, suppression campaigns, and large-scale land warfare, and far more numerous than the Banner forces

description.zh: 主要用于长江、运河与沿海作战及运输，1648年规模仍在扩充，面对南明与沿海势力时尤其关键
description.en: Used chiefly for operations and transport on the Yangzi, the Grand Canal, and coastal waters; still expanding in 1648 and especially important against Southern Ming and coastal forces

commanderInChief.zh: 顺治帝名义统帅，实际由摄政王多尔衮总领军国大政
commanderInChief.en: Nominally commanded by the Shunzhi Emperor, but in practice directed by the Prince Regent Dorgon

technology.zh: 军队技术处于东亚火器化战争的成熟阶段，广泛使用弓骑、刀矛、火绳枪、轻重火炮、攻城器械与骑步协同。清军继承后金机动骑战传统，并吸收明军炮兵、火器工匠与城池攻守经验，在大规模野战、围城战和河运后勤方面具备很强能力。
technology.en: Military technology stood in the mature East Asian gunpowder-war stage, with broad use of mounted archery, bladed weapons, spears, matchlocks, light and heavy cannon, siege equipment, and combined cavalry-infantry tactics. The Qing preserved the mobile cavalry traditions of the Later Jin while absorbing Ming artillery, firearms craftsmen, and siege expertise, giving them strong capacities in field warfare, sieges, and riverine logistics.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自南明残余政权及其地方拥护者、华南和西南未完全归附地区、沿海反清力量，以及北方草原与西北边疆潜在的不稳定因素。对俄接触虽尚未成为1648年的首要军事问题，但东北边境未来已存在潜在摩擦可能。
threats.en: The main threats came from remnant Southern Ming regimes and their local supporters, incompletely pacified regions in south and southwest China, anti-Qing coastal forces, and potential instability on the steppe and northwestern frontiers. Contact with Russia had not yet become the foremost military issue in 1648, but future friction on the northeastern frontier was already possible.

populationDescription.zh: 人口总量极大，但分布不均且深受明清鼎革影响。华北部分地区因战乱、饥荒与迁徙而人口受损，江南、东南与长江流域仍是最稠密、最富庶的人口带。满洲、蒙古与旗人群体在总人口中占比较小，但在军事与政治上地位极高。
populationDescription.en: The population was enormous but unevenly distributed and deeply affected by the Ming-Qing transition. Parts of north China suffered demographic losses from war, famine, and migration, while Jiangnan, the southeast, and the Yangzi basin remained the densest and richest population zones. Manchus, Mongols, and banner populations were numerically small within the whole but held disproportionately high military and political status.

socialClasses.zh: 上层包括皇室宗亲、旗人贵族、高级将领、大学士、总督巡抚与大地主大商人；中层包括地方士绅、生员、较富农、手工业主与中等商人；下层为小农、佃户、军户、工匠、雇工、运输劳工与城市贫民。另有旗民身份界线、降附群体与边疆部族社群等横向差异。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata included the imperial clan, banner nobles, senior commanders, grand secretaries, governors-general, governors, and great landlords and merchants; the middle strata comprised local gentry, degree students, better-off peasants, workshop owners, and middling traders; the lower strata consisted of smallholders, tenants, military households, artisans, hired laborers, transport workers, and the urban poor. Cross-cutting distinctions also existed between Bannermen and civilians, submitted populations, and frontier tribal communities.

allies.zh: 主要依靠归附蒙古诸部、朝鲜王朝的朝贡性服从与合作，以及部分地方汉族降臣降将构成的政治军事联盟网络
allies.en: Its principal partners were the submitted Mongol groups, the tributary submission and cooperation of Joseon Korea, and political-military networks formed by surrendered Han officials and commanders

enemies.zh: 南明诸政权及其地方支持者是首要敌人；同时对沿海拥明势力、部分西南地方军阀以及任何可能支援反清阵营的外部势力保持警惕
enemies.en: The various Southern Ming regimes and their local supporters were the primary enemies; the court also remained wary of pro-Ming coastal forces, some southwestern military strongmen, and any external power that might aid anti-Qing resistance

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以巩固征服成果和重建朝贡秩序为核心。对朝鲜与蒙古强调宗主与盟属关系；对日本尚无稳定直接关系；对中亚、藏地与西南边区采取审慎接触与间接控制；对欧洲海上势力仍以有限贸易认知和沿海防御视角看待。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on consolidating conquest and rebuilding a tributary order. Toward Korea and Mongolia the Qing emphasized suzerain and allied-subordinate ties; there was no stable direct relationship with Japan; toward Inner Asia, Tibet, and the southwestern marches it pursued cautious contact and indirect control; European maritime powers were still viewed mainly through the lenses of limited trade and coastal defense.

era.zh: 早期近代火器与官僚帝国时代
era.en: Early Modern gunpowder and bureaucratic-imperial age

keyInnovations.zh: 关键能力并非单一发明，而在于制度与技术综合：八旗组织动员、满汉双语档案行政、运河与漕运后勤体系、火绳枪与红夷炮等火器运用、攻城筑城工程、区域粮食市场整合，以及大型帝国对人口、赋税和军队的文书管理。
keyInnovations.en: The key advances were less single inventions than integrated capacities: Eight Banner mobilization, bilingual Manchu-Chinese archival administration, canal and grain-transport logistics, use of matchlocks and heavy cannon such as Hongyi-style artillery, siege and fortification engineering, integration of regional grain markets, and documentary management of population, taxation, and armies on an imperial scale.

strengths.zh: 清帝国在1648年的最大优势是强大的征服军事实力、组织严密的八旗—绿营双重体系、对明代行政遗产的迅速接收能力，以及北京作为政治军事中心的稳固控制。其统治集团既具草原-满洲军事传统，又善于借用汉地官僚、士绅和税收制度，因而扩张效率极高。
strengths.en: The Qing Empire’s greatest strengths in 1648 were its formidable conquest military, the well-organized dual Banner-Green Standard system, its rapid appropriation of the Ming administrative inheritance, and secure control of Beijing as a political-military center. Its ruling group combined steppe-Manchu military traditions with effective use of Han bureaucracy, gentry, and fiscal institutions, giving it exceptional expansionary efficiency.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于统一尚未完成，南方许多富庶地区仍处战乱或反复争夺之中；财政因战争支出而紧张；旗民、满汉之间的身份差异可能带来治理摩擦；大量降附官军的忠诚度、地方豪强的服从度及沿海控制力仍有不确定性。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in the unfinished conquest, with many rich southern regions still in war or contested control; finances were strained by military spending; status differences between Bannermen and civilians and between Manchus and Han could produce administrative friction; and the loyalty of many surrendered officials and troops, the obedience of local strongmen, and control of the coast remained uncertain.

outlook.zh: 前景总体非常强劲。若清廷能继续整合汉地财政、稳定江南税源、控制长江与沿海，并逐步将军事征服转化为常态化文官治理，则极可能完成全国统一并建立一个持续数代的强盛帝国。但若南方抵抗延长、财政失衡加剧或宫廷权力斗争失控，统一成本也会显著上升。
outlook.en: The overall outlook was very strong. If the Qing could continue integrating Chinese fiscal structures, secure the Jiangnan tax base, control the Yangzi and the coast, and gradually convert military conquest into regular civil governance, it was highly likely to complete unification and build a powerful empire lasting for generations. But if southern resistance persisted, fiscal imbalance worsened, or court power struggles escaped control, the cost of unification would rise sharply.

description.zh: 清廷在入关后迅速扩张，1648年正处于统一中国的大规模战争阶段。
description.en: After entering China proper, the Qing court was rapidly expanding and in 1648 was engaged in major campaigns to unify China.

governmentForm.zh: 以将军为核心、朝廷与幕府并存的封建武家政权
governmentForm.en: A feudal warrior government centered on the shogun, with the imperial court and bakufu coexisting

socialStructure.zh: 社会大体按身分秩序组织，主流观念为士农工商四民体制，另有神官、僧侣、公家及被排斥群体。各藩与幕府共同维持地方等级与治安，身份流动有限但并非完全冻结。
socialStructure.en: Society was broadly organized by status order, commonly expressed as the samurai-peasant-artisan-merchant hierarchy, alongside Shinto clergy, Buddhist clergy, court nobles, and outcaste communities. Domains and the bakufu jointly maintained local rank and order, and social mobility was limited though not entirely frozen.

rulingClass.zh: 德川将军家、旗本与御家人、亲藩与谱代大名，以及在京都保有礼仪地位的朝廷公家
rulingClass.en: The Tokugawa shogunal house, direct retainers such as hatamoto and gokenin, shinpan and fudai daimyo, and the Kyoto court nobility retaining ceremonial prestige

succession.zh: 将军职位原则上由德川宗家世袭，必要时可在御三家等德川近支中择继承人；大名领地亦多为家督世袭，但须经幕府认可。
succession.en: The shogunate was in principle hereditary within the main Tokugawa line, with successors drawn when necessary from close Tokugawa branches such as the Gosanke; daimyo domains were likewise generally hereditary but required bakufu confirmation.

structure.zh: 1648年的政治结构已较为稳固：名义上天皇仍为最高礼制权威，实际军政由江户幕府掌握。幕府通过老中、若年寄、寺社奉行、町奉行、勘定奉行等机构管理全国，并以武家诸法度、参勤交代、城郭与婚姻管制约束大名。全国并非完全中央集权，而是由幕府直辖地与诸藩构成的幕藩体制。
structure.en: By 1648 the political structure had become relatively stable: the emperor remained the supreme ceremonial authority in name, while actual military and administrative power rested with the Edo bakufu. The bakufu governed through bodies such as the rōjū, wakadoshiyori, jisha bugyō, machi bugyō, and kanjō bugyō, while controlling daimyo through the Laws for the Military Houses, sankin-kōtai, and restrictions on castles and marriage alliances. The realm was not fully centralized, but formed a bakuhan system combining shogunal lands and semi-autonomous domains.

function.zh: 统筹幕府政务，处理大名事务、全国性法令与对外重大决策
function.en: Coordinated bakufu government, handling daimyo affairs, realm-wide decrees, and major foreign decisions

function.zh: 管理旗本、御家人及江户城内日常武家事务
function.en: Managed hatamoto, gokenin, and the daily affairs of the warrior administration in Edo Castle

function.zh: 监督佛寺、神社、宗教纠纷及部分土地争议，并协助宗教管控
function.en: Oversaw Buddhist temples, Shinto shrines, religious disputes, and some land cases, while assisting religious regulation

function.zh: 掌管幕府财政、年贡征收、直辖地管理与仓储
function.en: Administered bakufu finances, land-tax collection, shogunal domain management, and granaries

function.zh: 管理江户市政、治安、消防、司法与商业秩序
function.en: Administered Edo urban affairs, policing, firefighting, justice, and commercial order

function.zh: 监视京都朝廷、公家与西国大名动向，维护畿内秩序
function.en: Monitored the Kyoto court, court nobles, and western daimyo activity while maintaining order in the Kinai region

function.zh: 统辖大坂城、仓储与畿内战略要地，监督重要商贸节点
function.en: Supervised Osaka Castle, storehouses, and a strategic Kinai strongpoint while overseeing a key commercial hub

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由幕府直辖地与诸藩并行。直辖地由代官、郡代管理村落和税收；诸藩由藩主及家臣团治理。村庄一般由名主、组头、百姓代负责基层事务，城市则由町年寄、町役人协助奉行实施管治。幕府通过人质制度化的参勤交代、道路网络、通行许可和法令监督地方。
localAdmin.en: Local administration combined shogunal territories and daimyo domains. Direct lands were overseen by daikan and gundai officials managing villages and taxation; domains were governed by daimyo and their retainers. Villages typically relied on nanushi, kumigashira, and peasant representatives for routine affairs, while cities used neighborhood elders and town officials under magistrates. The bakufu supervised local power through sankin-kōtai, road networks, travel permits, and legal regulations.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系是武家法、幕府法令、惯习法与身份秩序的结合。核心规范包括《武家诸法度》及针对朝廷、寺社、农民和城市的各类规定。司法依身分与辖区而异，武士、百姓、寺社常分别受不同机关裁断。审判强调供述、调解、连坐责任与维持秩序，而非近代意义上的普遍平等法权。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined warrior law, bakufu edicts, customary law, and status hierarchy. Core norms included the Laws for the Military Houses and various regulations directed at the court, temples and shrines, peasants, and towns. Justice varied by status and jurisdiction, with samurai, commoners, and religious institutions often judged by different authorities. Proceedings emphasized confession, mediation, collective responsibility, and maintenance of order rather than modern notions of equal legal rights.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政基础主要是稻米年贡，以石高制衡量土地生产力。幕府直辖地直接征收，诸藩则自行课征以维持藩政。另有劳役、特许商业税、港口和运输相关征收、矿山收入及城镇杂税。武士俸禄多以米为基准，米价波动因此直接影响财政稳定。
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal base was primarily the rice land tax, assessed through the kokudaka system measuring productive capacity. Shogunal lands paid directly to the bakufu, while domains levied their own dues to sustain domain administration. Additional burdens included labor service, licensed commercial taxes, port and transport charges, mining income, and miscellaneous urban levies. Samurai stipends were commonly denominated in rice, so fluctuations in rice prices directly affected fiscal stability.

religion.zh: 宗教生活以佛教与神道并存为主，二者长期习合。幕府在镇压基督教后强化寺请制度，要求民众隶属于寺院以证明非基督徒。儒学尤其是朱子学在武家政治伦理中日益重要，但尚未完全取代佛教与神道的社会功能。
religion.en: Religious life was dominated by the coexistence of Buddhism and Shinto, long fused in syncretic practice. After suppressing Christianity, the bakufu strengthened the temple-registration system, requiring households to affiliate with temples as proof of non-Christian status. Confucian thought, especially Neo-Confucianism, was becoming increasingly important in warrior political ethics, though it had not displaced the social functions of Buddhism and Shinto.

philosophy.zh: 主导思想为朱子学化的儒家秩序观，强调名分、忠孝、等级和治国伦理；同时保有武家传统、佛教因果观与神道祭祀观念。思想界尚未完全统一，林罗山一系学术对幕府正统化影响显著。
philosophy.en: The leading philosophy was a Neo-Confucian vision of ordered society emphasizing status, loyalty, filiality, hierarchy, and statecraft, while warrior traditions, Buddhist ideas of karma, and Shinto ritual concepts remained influential. Intellectual life was not fully uniform, but the Hayashi scholarly line had a marked impact on bakufu ideological legitimation.

writingSystem.zh: 书写系统结合汉字与假名。官方文书、学术著述和外交汉文大量使用，日常书写则常以和汉混淆文体、平假名和片假名并用。
writingSystem.en: The writing system combined Chinese characters with kana. Official documents, scholarship, and much diplomatic writing made heavy use of classical Chinese, while everyday writing commonly employed mixed Sino-Japanese styles alongside hiragana and katakana.

culturalAchievements.zh: 17世纪中叶的江户日本已出现和平秩序下的文化整合。城下町与交通干线发展推动出版、教育和都市消费增长。俳谐、茶道、能乐、书画、庭园、美术工艺及武家礼法继续成熟；京都、大坂与江户逐步形成互补的文化中心。对外虽收紧往来，但通过长崎、对马、萨摩、松前等窗口仍吸收医学、器物、书籍和海外资讯。
culturalAchievements.en: By the mid-seventeenth century, Edo Japan had developed notable cultural integration under conditions of peace. Castle towns and transport corridors fostered growth in publishing, education, and urban consumption. Linked verse, tea culture, Noh, calligraphy, painting, gardens, decorative arts, and warrior etiquette all continued to mature; Kyoto, Osaka, and Edo were increasingly becoming complementary cultural centers. Although foreign contact was restricted, windows through Nagasaki, Tsushima, Satsuma, and Matsumae still brought in medicine, objects, books, and overseas information.

languageFamily.zh: 日语系
languageFamily.en: Japonic

gdpDescription.zh: 江户初期日本经济规模在当时世界属较大体量，以高密度农业、区域市场和城下町消费为基础。产出以稻作为核心，但丝织、棉布、纸张、木材、盐、渔业、酒造、手工业和矿业也颇重要。度量以现代意义精确换算困难，因此以石米当量作近似表达更符合时代结构。
gdpDescription.en: Early Edo Japan possessed a large economy by contemporary world standards, grounded in intensive agriculture, regional markets, and castle-town consumption. Rice stood at the center of output, but silk, cotton cloth, paper, timber, salt, fisheries, brewing, handicrafts, and mining were also important. Exact conversion into modern units is difficult, so a koku-rice-equivalent estimate better reflects the structure of the period.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、桑蚕与丝织、棉作与纺织、渔业、木材与造船、盐业、清酒酿造、纸业、陶瓷、漆器、金银铜矿开采与冶炼、城下町手工业与运输业
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, sericulture and silk weaving, cotton cultivation and textiles, fisheries, timber and shipbuilding, salt production, sake brewing, papermaking, ceramics, lacquerware, gold-silver-copper mining and smelting, and castle-town handicrafts and transport services

tradeGoods.zh: 国内流通以米、棉布、丝绸、纸、木材、海产、盐、酒、油、陶瓷和日用手工业品为主；对外贸易经长崎等口岸输出银、铜、海产、工艺品，输入生丝、丝织品、药材、糖、鹿皮、书籍与少量火器相关物资。
tradeGoods.en: Domestic circulation centered on rice, cotton cloth, silk, paper, timber, marine products, salt, sake, oil, ceramics, and everyday handicrafts; overseas trade through Nagasaki and other channels exported silver, copper, marine goods, and crafts, while importing raw silk, silk textiles, medicines, sugar, deerskins, books, and limited firearms-related materials.

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富分化明显。农民家庭财富主要表现为土地使用权、粮食余裕、农具和劳动力；商人和手工业者则积累现金、货物与债权。武士阶层名义地位高，但相当部分依赖固定俸禄，实际流动财富常不如富裕町人。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Peasant wealth rested mainly on land-use rights, grain surpluses, tools, and labor capacity; merchants and artisans accumulated cash, inventories, and credit claims. The samurai ranked highest in status, but many depended on fixed stipends and often possessed less liquid wealth than prosperous townspeople.

economicSystem.zh: 经济制度是以农业年贡为根基、货币交换与市场网络快速扩大的封建市场经济。政治上强调身分与管制，经济上则容许并依赖商人、运输和信贷体系发展。幕府限制对外贸易和海外活动，但国内市场一体化程度持续提高。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a feudal market economy rooted in agrarian taxation but increasingly shaped by monetary exchange and expanding market networks. Politically it stressed status and regulation, yet economically it allowed and relied upon merchants, transport, and credit systems. The bakufu restricted foreign trade and overseas movement, but domestic market integration continued to advance.

source.zh: 罚没、贡纳与其他临时收入
source.en: Confiscations, tribute, and other extraordinary receipts

category.zh: 江户城、行政与官署运作
京都朝廷、寺社与礼仪支出

category.en: Policing, coastal defense, and frontier oversight

treasuryDescription.zh: 幕府金库储备在1648年前后总体稳健，受益于和平秩序、较强的直辖地收入与此前金银矿收益，但现金与米储并不完全等同，且需面对火灾、饥馑、工程和政治赏赐等突发负担。财政稳健更多体现为幕府信用与征收能力，而非现代意义上的统一现金预算。
treasuryDescription.en: The bakufu treasury around 1648 was generally sound, benefiting from peace, substantial income from shogunal lands, and earlier precious-metal production, but cash reserves and rice stocks were not identical and had to cover sudden burdens such as fires, famine, construction, and political rewards. Fiscal strength rested as much on bakufu credit and extraction capacity as on a unified cash budget in the modern sense.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持秩序和武家支配为先，强调稳定征收、储粮、节用与控制大名经济力量。幕府避免持续性对外军事扩张，借此降低超额军费；同时通过参勤交代把部分政治成本转移给大名。总体上属保守、重储备、以农税为本的财政模式。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized order and warrior rule, emphasizing stable collection, grain reserves, frugality, and constraints on daimyo economic power. The bakufu avoided sustained overseas military expansion, reducing extraordinary military costs, while sankin-kōtai shifted part of the political burden onto daimyo. Overall it was a conservative, reserve-minded fiscal regime grounded in agrarian taxation.

name.zh: 幕府直属步兵与城卫军

description.zh: 由旗本、御家人及其足轻组成，负责江户城警卫、将军护卫、治安与战时核心动员
description.en: Composed of hatamoto, gokenin, and their ashigaru, serving Edo Castle security, shogunal guard duty, policing, and wartime core mobilization

description.zh: 由各大名维持的武士与附属步兵构成，是全国动员的主体；平时分散驻于城下町和领地
description.en: Made up of samurai and attached infantry maintained by daimyo, these formed the main body of nationwide mobilization and were dispersed across castle towns and domains in peacetime

description.zh: 规模有限，以巡察、海岸守备、运输与港湾控制为主，不是远洋海军
description.en: Limited in scale, focused on patrol, coastal defense, transport, and harbor control rather than blue-water naval warfare

description.zh: 战时可由各地武士从者、足轻与辅助人力补充，但训练与装备水平不一
description.en: In wartime these could be supplemented by retainers, ashigaru, and local auxiliaries, though training and equipment varied considerably

commanderInChief.zh: 德川家光
commanderInChief.en: Tokugawa Iemitsu

technology.zh: 军事实力建立在战国时代遗产之上，火绳枪、长枪、弓、刀和坚固城郭体系并用。1648年时火器制造与运用经验仍在，但长期和平使大规模野战训练减少。海军技术偏向沿岸航运与治安用途，幕府并压制远洋造船与海外军事冒险。
technology.en: Military capacity rested on the inheritance of the Sengoku era, combining matchlocks, spears, bows, swords, and strong castle systems. By 1648 experience in firearms manufacture and use remained, but prolonged peace had reduced large-scale field training. Naval technology was oriented toward coastal transport and security, and the bakufu suppressed ocean-going shipbuilding and overseas military ventures.

threats.zh: 主要威胁并非持续外敌入侵，而是内部藩镇不稳、基督教残余、沿海走私与海盗、北方边地管理，以及东亚大陆局势变化带来的不确定性。清朝取代明朝的进程和朝鲜通信使体系中的外交平衡，使幕府需谨慎处理对外情报与沿岸防务。
threats.en: The principal threats were not sustained foreign invasion but internal domain instability, residual Christianity, coastal smuggling and piracy, northern frontier management, and uncertainty created by shifts on the East Asian mainland. The Qing replacement of the Ming and the diplomatic balancing reflected in relations involving Korea required the bakufu to handle foreign intelligence and coastal defense carefully.

populationDescription.zh: 1648年前后的日本人口大致处于恢复和增长阶段，总量约一千八百万属合理估计。人口主体为农村农民，但城下町、港町和商业城市持续扩张。由于长年和平、农业开发和治安稳定，人口分布较为稠密，尤其在畿内、东海道和若干平原地区。
populationDescription.en: Japan's population around 1648 was broadly in a phase of recovery and growth, and a total of about 18 million is a reasonable estimate. The great majority lived in the countryside, but castle towns, ports, and commercial cities were expanding steadily. Long peace, agrarian development, and stable order produced relatively dense settlement, especially in the Kinai, along the Tōkaidō, and in major plains.

socialClasses.zh: 主要社会阶层包括将军家与大名、旗本御家人等武士阶层、农民、工匠、商人、公家、僧侣与神官，以及处于边缘的被差别对待群体。名义上士居四民之首，但在和平时期，商人和大城市手工业者的经济影响力逐步增强。
socialClasses.en: The main social classes included the shogunal and daimyo elite, the samurai stratum of hatamoto and gokenin, peasants, artisans, merchants, court nobles, Buddhist and Shinto clergy, and marginalized outcaste communities. In formal ideology the samurai stood at the top of the commoner order, but under peace the economic influence of merchants and urban artisans gradually increased.

allies.zh: 日本在1648年并无近代意义上的正式军事同盟。相对友好且制度化的对外关系主要包括与朝鲜王朝通过对马藩维持的通信使关系、与琉球经萨摩藩控制下的间接往来，以及与荷兰东印度公司和中国商人在长崎的受限贸易联系。
allies.en: Japan had no formal military allies in the modern sense in 1648. Its more regular and relatively stable external relations included the Korean Joseon court through Tsushima-mediated embassy exchange, indirect dealings with Ryukyu under Satsuma's control, and restricted trade contacts with the Dutch East India Company and Chinese merchants at Nagasaki.

enemies.zh: 无公开长期交战的国家级宿敌，但幕府将天主教势力、伊比利亚传教网络及任何可能破坏锁国秩序的外来干预视为潜在敌对力量。对东亚大陆政局则保持谨慎观望。
enemies.en: There was no openly warring state-level archenemy, but the bakufu regarded Catholic influence, Iberian missionary networks, and any foreign intervention that might undermine the seclusion order as potentially hostile. It also watched developments on the East Asian mainland with caution.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策核心是限制而非彻底隔绝，即后世所谓锁国体制的成形阶段。幕府严控日本人出海、限制传教、集中对外贸易于少数口岸与中介藩，同时保留必要的外交与资讯渠道，以确保国内安定、削弱大名自主外交并防止海外势力渗透。
foreignPolicy.en: The core of foreign policy was restriction rather than total isolation, representing the formative stage of what later came to be called the sakoku order. The bakufu tightly controlled overseas travel by Japanese, banned missionary activity, and concentrated foreign trade in a few ports and intermediary domains, while preserving necessary diplomatic and informational channels to secure domestic stability, weaken daimyo autonomy in foreign affairs, and prevent overseas infiltration.

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术特点包括成熟的稻作水利、全国驿路与交通组织、城郭建筑、木构都市工程、火绳枪制造与使用、金银铜矿开采冶炼、印刷出版、和纸生产、精细手工业及较高水平的会计与仓储管理。虽然幕府限制部分海洋技术和海外扩张，但国内土木、农业与商业技术持续进步。
keyInnovations.en: Key technological strengths included mature irrigated rice agriculture, national road and relay systems, castle engineering, wooden urban construction, matchlock manufacture and use, gold-silver-copper mining and smelting, printing and publishing, papermaking, refined handicrafts, and relatively advanced accounting and granary management. Although the bakufu restricted certain maritime technologies and overseas expansion, domestic advances in civil engineering, agriculture, and commerce continued.

strengths.zh: 1648年的江户幕府日本最大优势在于内战结束后的长期稳定、对大名的有效制度化控制、较高农业生产率、不断整合的国内市场，以及能够把传统武家统治与城市经济增长结合起来。江户、大坂、京都构成互补核心，国家有较强动员与治安能力。
strengths.en: The greatest strengths of Edo Japan in 1648 were long-term stability after civil war, effective institutional control over daimyo, relatively high agricultural productivity, an increasingly integrated domestic market, and the ability to combine traditional warrior rule with urban economic growth. Edo, Osaka, and Kyoto formed complementary cores, and the state possessed strong capacities for mobilization and public order.

weaknesses.zh: 主要弱点在于制度高度依赖身分秩序与米税体系，财政对米价和收成敏感；武士俸禄固定，长期易受货币经济侵蚀；地方藩政差异大，中央并非完全一体化。对外信息渠道虽存在，但有限且受控制，可能降低对全球变化的反应速度。
weaknesses.en: Its main weaknesses lay in a system heavily dependent on status order and rice taxation, leaving finance sensitive to harvests and rice prices; samurai stipends were fixed and thus vulnerable in the long run to monetization; and domain governance varied widely, so the polity was not fully integrated. External information channels existed but were limited and controlled, potentially slowing responses to wider global changes.

outlook.zh: 短期前景总体稳定且偏向繁荣。只要幕府持续维持参勤交代、宗教控制、财政储备与大名秩序，日本将在未来数十年继续享有相对和平与经济扩张。但中长期看，武士财政压力、城乡不平衡、人口增长与市场化带来的新矛盾将逐渐浮现。
outlook.en: The short-term outlook was broadly stable and favorable to prosperity. So long as the bakufu maintained sankin-kōtai, religious controls, fiscal reserves, and daimyo discipline, Japan was likely to enjoy continuing peace and economic expansion over the coming decades. In the medium to long term, however, samurai fiscal strain, urban-rural imbalance, population growth, and tensions generated by commercialization would gradually emerge.

description.zh: 德川幕府巩固锁国体制与大名秩序，日本进入相对稳定的江户时代。
description.en: The Tokugawa bakufu consolidated the sakoku order and daimyo hierarchy, ushering in relative stability in Edo Japan.

governmentForm.zh: 以儒家官僚制为核心的君主制，国王与文官士大夫共治
governmentForm.en: A Confucian bureaucratic monarchy in which the king ruled in concert with a civilian scholar-official elite

socialStructure.zh: 社会以身分秩序为基础，由两班士族居上，其下为中人、良民平民、贱民与奴婢；宗族、乡约与科举共同维系地方与中央秩序
socialStructure.en: Society was structured by status hierarchy, with the yangban elite at the top, followed by technical middle groups, commoners, and low-status groups including slaves; lineages, local compacts, and the examination system linked local society to the state

rulingClass.zh: 以两班文官集团和中央高官家族为核心的士大夫阶层
rulingClass.en: The scholar-official elite, especially the yangban civil bureaucracy and leading court lineages

succession.zh: 王位原则上传嫡长子，由李氏王室世袭，但须经朝廷确认并受宗法礼制约束
succession.en: The throne was hereditary within the House of Yi, ideally passing to the legitimate eldest son, though court confirmation and dynastic ritual norms shaped succession

structure.zh: 1648年的朝鲜仍沿用《经国大典》确立的中央集权体制。国王居于最高统治地位，下设议政府、六曹、三司及诸专门机构。政治运行深受西人与南人等党争影响，但总体官僚框架稳定，地方由八道观察使、府尹、牧使、郡守、县监等管理。
structure.en: In 1648 Joseon still operated under the centralized framework codified in the Gyeongguk daejeon. The king stood at the apex, supported by the State Council, the Six Ministries, the Three Offices, and specialized agencies. Politics was heavily affected by factional competition, especially among Westerners and Southerners, but the overall bureaucratic structure remained stable. Locally, the eight provinces were governed by governors, magistrates, and county officials.

function.zh: 统筹国家政务，协调各曹，向国王进言并审议重大行政事务
function.en: Coordinated major state affairs, supervised ministry-level administration, advised the king, and deliberated important policy matters

function.zh: 掌官员任免、考课、品阶与文官人事管理
function.en: Managed appointments, evaluations, ranks, and personnel affairs of officials

function.zh: 掌户籍、田赋、财政收支、仓储与部分贡纳事务
function.en: Handled household registers, land tax, fiscal revenue and expenditure, granaries, and parts of tribute administration

function.zh: 掌国家礼仪、祭祀、外交文书、学校与科举
function.en: Oversaw state ritual, sacrifices, diplomatic protocol, education, and the civil examinations

function.zh: 掌军籍、兵役、军官任命、部分防务调度与武备事务
function.en: Managed military registers, service obligations, officer appointments, and parts of defense administration and armaments

function.zh: 掌司法审判、刑狱、法令执行与案件覆核
function.en: Oversaw judicial proceedings, penal matters, implementation of laws, and review of cases

function.zh: 掌土木工程、官营手工业、道路桥梁、山林与部分水利
function.en: Managed public works, state craft production, roads and bridges, forests, and some water-control works

function.zh: 监察官员风纪，弹劾贪污失职，维护官僚伦理秩序
function.en: Monitored official conduct, impeached corruption and misconduct, and upheld bureaucratic morality

function.zh: 对国王与政府政策进行谏诤，纠正政治失当
function.en: Remonstrated with the king and government, seeking to correct political errors

function.zh: 掌经筵、文翰、史学讨论与王室顾问事务
function.en: Handled royal lectures, drafting, classical scholarship, and advisory functions for the throne

function.zh: 传达王命，管理奏章与王室日常政务沟通
function.en: Transmitted royal orders and managed memorials and day-to-day communication of the court

function.zh: 记录朝政、编修实录与保存国家史料
function.en: Recorded government affairs, compiled annals, and preserved official historical records

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以八道为骨架，下辖府、牧、郡、县。中央派遣观察使巡察各道，地方守令承担征税、司法、治安、仓储与赈济。乡村层面依托里、坊、面等基层组织及地方乡吏运作，但地方士族与宗族势力对实际治理影响很大。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized through the eight provinces, which were subdivided into prefectures, counties, and districts. Provincial governors represented the center, while local magistrates handled taxation, justice, order, granaries, and relief. At the village level, administration relied on basic territorial units and clerks, though local yangban lineages exercised considerable practical influence.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以成文法、王命和礼教并用，核心依据为《经国大典》及其后续补编，并参照《大明律》。司法重视纲常秩序、家族伦理与官僚等级，刑罚包括笞、杖、徒、流、死等，但审理中常受身分差异与政治派系影响。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined codified law, royal edicts, and Confucian moral norms. Its core rested on the Gyeongguk daejeon and later supplements, with strong influence from the Great Ming Code. Justice emphasized social hierarchy, family ethics, and bureaucratic order; punishments ranged from flogging and beating to penal servitude, exile, and execution, though judgments were often shaped by status and factional politics.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以田赋、贡物、徭役和军役义务为基础。战后财政压力使国家更加依赖大同法在部分地区的推行，以米布等统一征收替代繁杂土贡。1648年大同法尚未全国普及，但已在京畿、江原、忠清、全罗等地区产生显著影响，税制正从实物杂贡向较统一的赋役体系转变。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was based on land tax, local tribute, corvée, and military service obligations. Postwar fiscal pressure increased reliance on the Daedong reform in some regions, replacing diverse local tribute items with more standardized payments in rice or cloth. In 1648 the reform had not yet reached the entire kingdom, but it was already significant in Gyeonggi, Gangwon, Chungcheong, and Jeolla, marking a gradual shift from fragmented tribute obligations to a more unified tax regime.

religion.zh: 国家正统为儒教礼制与祖先祭祀，民间则兼有佛教、巫俗与风水信仰。朝廷自称尊崇程朱理学，对佛教保持限制态度，但寺院在山区社会和战后祈福活动中仍具影响。
religion.en: Official orthodoxy centered on Confucian ritual order and ancestral worship, while popular religion also included Buddhism, shamanic practices, and geomancy. The court upheld Neo-Confucianism and kept Buddhism under restriction, yet monasteries still retained influence in mountain communities and postwar devotional life.

philosophy.zh: 主流思想为朱子学，强调名分、礼制、忠孝与修身治国。1648年前后学术讨论也涉及心性论、义理政治与华夷观，尤其在明清鼎革后，朝鲜士林愈发强化“尊周思明”的文化自我定位。
philosophy.en: The dominant philosophy was Zhu Xi Neo-Confucianism, stressing hierarchy, ritual, loyalty, filial piety, and moral self-cultivation as the basis of government. Around 1648 scholarly debate also engaged questions of mind and principle, righteous politics, and the civilized-barbarian distinction; after the Ming-Qing transition, Korean literati increasingly defined Joseon as a guardian of orthodox civilization and Ming cultural legitimacy.

writingSystem.zh: 官方书写主要使用汉文，王朝行政、奏章与学术著述皆以文言为主；训民正音即谚文已广泛存在于民间、女性书写和部分实用文本中，但在上层官僚文化中的地位仍次于汉文。
writingSystem.en: Classical Chinese remained the principal official writing system for administration, memorials, and elite scholarship. Hunminjeongeum, or vernacular Korean script, was widely present in popular use, women's writing, and practical texts, but its status in upper bureaucratic culture was still secondary to literary Chinese.

culturalAchievements.zh: 壬辰与丙子战后，朝鲜继续保持高度发达的史学、儒学经学、木版印刷、地图编纂、白瓷工艺与宗族文集传统。宫廷与士林重视实录、日记、家乘和地方志编修，陶瓷与书画在战后恢复中重建品质。医学、农书与礼书的整理亦体现出重建国家秩序的文化努力。
culturalAchievements.en: After the Imjin and Manchu wars, Joseon maintained highly developed traditions in historiography, Confucian scholarship, woodblock printing, cartography, white porcelain, and lineage compilation. Court and literati circles valued the compilation of annals, diaries, genealogies, and local gazetteers, while ceramics and painting gradually recovered quality in the postwar period. The editing of medical, agricultural, and ritual works also reflected broader efforts to rebuild state order.

languageFamily.zh: 朝鲜语，通常归类为朝鲜语系或语言孤立体
languageFamily.en: Korean, commonly treated as the Koreanic language family or a language isolate

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 朝鲜经济以农业为绝对基础，1648年仍处于战后恢复阶段。总体产出在东亚中属中等规模，远逊于清与日本主要地区，但以人口规模、稻作与旱作并行体系、官仓和地方市场网络支撑了一个稳定王国。GDP估算反映的是以银两折算的总产出，并非近代国民收入统计。
gdpDescription.en: The Joseon economy was overwhelmingly agrarian and in 1648 was still in postwar recovery. Overall output was moderate by East Asian standards, far smaller than the Qing realm and the wealthiest regions of Japan, yet a stable kingdom was sustained by population size, mixed wet-rice and dry-field farming, official granaries, and regional market networks. The GDP estimate is a silver-denominated approximation of total output rather than a modern national-income measure.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、稻米与杂粮种植、棉布与麻布纺织、陶瓷烧造、盐业、渔业、造纸、木版印刷、冶铁与地方手工业
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, rice and mixed grain cultivation, cotton and hemp textiles, ceramics, salt production, fisheries, papermaking, woodblock printing, ironworking, and local handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 主要流通与贸易品包括米、豆、棉布、麻布、人参、纸张、书籍、陶瓷、海产、木材、铁器及少量药材；对外贸易中尤以人参与皮张、金属、书籍和工艺品较受重视
tradeGoods.en: Major goods in circulation and trade included rice, beans, cotton cloth, hemp cloth, ginseng, paper, books, ceramics, marine products, timber, ironware, and some medicinal materials; in foreign trade, ginseng, hides, metals, books, and crafted goods were especially valued

name.zh: 布币、米谷与杂用铜钱并行
name.en: Cloth money, grain payments, and limited copper cash

unitName.en: bolts of cloth and seok of grain

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距显著。两班家族拥有土地、奴婢、书籍和宗族网络，是财富与地位核心；普通农户多接近自给，常受赋役、歉收和债务压力；战后流民和失地者数量仍不可忽视。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Yangban lineages held land, slaves, books, and lineage networks and formed the core of wealth and status; ordinary peasant households often hovered near subsistence and were vulnerable to taxes, poor harvests, and debt; postwar displaced and land-poor populations remained significant.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 国家控制与地方市场并存的农业经济。土地与赋役制度由国家登记管理，但大量实际分配受地方士族、佃作关系和市场交换影响。官方对外贸易高度受限，主要通过对清朝贡体系、对日通信与釜山倭馆贸易进行管理。
economicSystem.en: The economy combined state supervision with local market exchange in an agrarian framework. Land and tax obligations were registered by the state, but actual distribution was shaped heavily by local elites, tenancy, and market transactions. Foreign trade was tightly regulated and conducted mainly through the tributary relationship with Qing China, diplomatic-commercial contacts with Japan, and trade through the Waegwan at Busan.

source.zh: 贡纳折收与大同法相关收入
官营手工业、盐业与杂项收入
source.en: State handicrafts, salt, and miscellaneous income

source.zh: 军役折纳与户籍相关征收
source.en: Commuted military obligations and household-related collections
Transit dues, market taxes, and other fees

category.en: Court, ritual, and royal household expenses
Public works, postal stations, and administrative miscellany

treasuryDescription.zh: 朝鲜国库并非单纯银库，而是米谷、布帛、铜钱、军需、仓储储备与账面折银并存。1648年财政总体恢复到可维持稳定运作的水平，但仍容易受歉收、疫病、边防压力和党争导致的征收效率低下所冲击。
treasuryDescription.en: The Joseon treasury was not a purely silver reserve but a combination of grain, cloth, copper cash, military supplies, granary stocks, and silver-equivalent accounting. By 1648 public finance had recovered enough for stable operation, but it remained vulnerable to bad harvests, disease, frontier pressure, and reduced collection efficiency caused by factional politics.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在战后恢复、维持军备、稳定仓储与推广较统一的赋役折纳。朝廷倾向于节制宫廷开支、整顿田籍和扩大大同法覆盖，但改革推进受地方既得利益和官僚派系掣肘。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on postwar recovery, maintenance of defense, stabilization of granaries, and wider use of more standardized commuted taxation. The court tended toward restraining court expenses, repairing land registers, and extending the Daedong reform, but reform was constrained by local vested interests and bureaucratic factionalism.

name.zh: 中央禁军与京城守备军

description.zh: 负责王宫、汉城与核心行政区域防务，包括训练都监等战后整编形成的精锐火器部队与宿卫兵力
description.en: Responsible for the defense of the palace, Hanseong, and the core administrative zone, including elite firearm troops and guard units reorganized after the wars

description.zh: 由各道、府、郡、县军额构成，承担城防、治安与紧急征发任务，是战时动员主力
description.en: Made up of registered provincial forces in prefectures and counties, handling fortress defense, local security, and emergency mobilization; they formed the core wartime manpower pool

description.zh: 主要部署于北方与交通要冲，用于警戒边境、传递军报和快速反应，但规模与质量不及北方游牧强国
description.en: Primarily deployed in the north and at strategic routes for frontier watch, dispatch carrying, and rapid response, though smaller and less capable than the cavalry of northern steppe powers

description.zh: 负责沿海巡防、港湾警戒和交通护送。壬辰战争后水军传统仍存，但1648年已较战争高峰期缩减
description.en: Handled coastal patrol, harbor defense, and escort duties. The naval tradition survived from the Imjin War, though by 1648 it was reduced from wartime peak levels

description.zh: 装备火绳枪、佛郎机炮及各类火器，承担守城、营阵火力和训练任务，是战后军事改革的重要成果
description.en: Equipped with matchlocks, light cannon, and other firearms, these troops provided fortress firepower, battlefield support, and training, representing an important result of postwar military reform

commanderInChief.zh: 仁祖国王为名义最高统帅，兵曹、训练都监与诸将领分掌军政军令
commanderInChief.en: King Injo was the nominal commander-in-chief, while the Ministry of War, the Military Training Agency, and senior commanders shared military administration and command

technology.zh: 朝鲜军队处于火器逐渐制度化的阶段，普遍使用火绳枪、弓箭、长枪、刀牌和各式城防火炮。海防与守城技术较成熟，军械制造受明制影响深，但后勤、骑兵与野战协同仍较弱。
technology.en: Joseon's military stood at a stage where firearms were becoming institutionalized. Matchlocks, bows, spears, swords and shields, and various fortress artillery were commonly used. Coastal and fortress defense techniques were relatively mature, and armaments were strongly influenced by Ming models, but logistics, cavalry capability, and open-field coordination remained weaker.

threats.zh: 首要威胁来自满清的宗主压力与北方边境安全，其次是对倭乱重演的长期戒备。虽然1648年对日关系较壬辰战争时期稳定，但沿海防务仍被重视；国内则需防范饥荒、流民与局部叛乱引发的治安风险。
threats.en: The primary threat came from Qing suzerain pressure and the security of the northern frontier, followed by the long-term fear of renewed Japanese aggression. Although relations with Japan were more stable than during the Imjin War, coastal defense remained important; domestically, famine, displaced people, and localized unrest posed additional security risks.
